overview: This appears to be an Eastern newt (Notophthalmus viridescens), which is a type of salamander. It looks like a juvenile in the "eft" stage, which is terrestrial, or a brown-colored adult. Newts are specialized salamanders known for having rougher skin and aquatic larval stages. These amphibians are gentle but secrete toxins from their skin for defense. NC Wildlife (.gov) +3 4 sites Is this a Froglet or a salamander? : r/herpetology

This appears to be an Eastern newt (Notophthalmus viridescens), which is a type of salamander.
It looks like a juvenile in the "eft" stage, which is terrestrial, or a brown-colored adult.
Newts are specialized salamanders known for having rougher skin and aquatic larval stages.
These amphibians are gentle but secrete toxins from their skin for defense.
